Executive Summary

The Dollarized State at War — Unique Mechanism

🔒 Dollarization Architecture (Since 2000)

Ecuador adopted the US Dollar in January 2000 following the collapse of the sucre. The Banco Central del Ecuador has no monetary policy whatsoever — it cannot set interest rates, conduct QE, print money, or devalue. The BCE is purely a fiscal supervisor and banking regulator.

This means Ecuador's risk signals are entirely fiscal and political, not monetary. When fiscal deficits widen, Ecuador has only three options: cut spending (politically catastrophic), raise revenue (oil/taxes), or borrow internationally (default risk elevated given 2008 and 2020 precedent).

Without a monetary escape valve, dollarization amplifies the severity of fiscal crises. Ecuador's bond yields (currently ~12.8%) reflect this: junk-rated, high-yield, with two defaults in five years making creditors deeply skeptical.

⚡ The Narco State Emergency — Unprecedented in 56 Canaries

No other canary in the CivilisationOS network combines: (1) full dollarization eliminating monetary buffer; (2) a state of war with narco organizations that control key infrastructure; (3) declining oil production from a democratic referendum halting drilling.

The assassination of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio (August 2023) and the Noboa government's formal "Internal Armed Conflict" declaration (January 2024) represent institutional failure at the sovereign level. Los Lobos and Choneros are not just street-level criminal organizations — they have effectively captured Guayaquil port (Ecuador's primary trade gateway) and threaten Esmeraldas oil refinery operations.

KC_02 is a single trigger: any port capture, presidential assassination, or SOE expansion forces ECRI to ELEVATED minimum regardless of composite score.

ECRI Formula — Dollarised Fiscal-Political Risk Index

ECRI = KC_01×0.30 + KC_02×0.25 + KC_03×0.20 + KC_04×0.15 + KC_05×0.10

Single trigger: KC_02 ≥0.60 forces ECRI to ELEVATED minimum regardless of composite.

ECRI History — Jan–Jul 2026

⚡ KC_02 — Narco Violence & State of Emergency (SINGLE TRIGGER)

⚡ SINGLE TRIGGER — KC_02 Score ≥0.60 = Immediate ECRI ELEVATED Override

Current KC_02 = 0.48. The trigger fires at 0.60. Current events — SOE ongoing, port at risk, prison system captured — place Ecuador 12 points below the single trigger threshold. Any escalation event (port capture, assassination, SOE expansion) crosses this threshold immediately.

The Narco Organizations

Los Lobos — Largest criminal org; controls Guayaquil port northern zone and has extensive Esmeraldas province presence (oil infrastructure access). Formed from remnants of Colombian FARC and local criminal networks.


Choneros — Historically dominant, now in violent conflict with Los Lobos. Controls major Guayaquil southern port areas and Manabí province. Prison control enables extortion and recruitment networks.


Tiguerones — Smaller but growing; associated with Mexican cartel networks (Sinaloa/CJNG). Primary cocaine transit operations.

Key Timeline Events (KC_02)

Aug 2023 — Fernando Villavicencio, presidential candidate who publicly named narco organizations, assassinated in Quito. Unprecedented in Ecuadorian history.


Jan 2024 — President Noboa declares "Internal Armed Conflict". Military deployed to streets. 22 criminal organizations designated as terrorist entities.


Jan 2024 — El Inca prison live TV takeover by armed gang. Guayaquil port under active threat from Los Lobos extortion operations.


Q1-Q3 2025 — State of emergency extended multiple times. Homicide rate peaked ~45.8/100k (2023), declining to ~35/100k (2026) with military operations.


Q3 2026 — SOE continues; Guayaquil port partially operational but under persistent extortion threat from narco organizations.

KC_01 + KC_03 — Fiscal & Oil Intelligence

Dollarization Fiscal Trap

Ecuador's dollarization eliminates the devaluation safety valve. Every fiscal crisis becomes a debt crisis in a way that countries with sovereign currencies can avoid. Ecuador's fiscal balance has been negative every year since 2013. Current deficit ~4.2% GDP.


Oil revenue (Petroecuador exports) provides ~30–35% of fiscal revenue. Declining production directly compresses the government's only significant export income source.

Yasuni ITT Block — The Oil Referendum

In August 2023, Ecuadorian voters approved a referendum halting oil drilling in the ITT block (Ishpingo–Tambococha–Tiputini) inside Yasuní National Park. This removes ~170,000 bpd of future production potential from an already declining field.


Current production: ~430k bpd (Q3 2026), down from 545k peak (2019). KC_03 threshold: below 400k bpd = score 0.55; below 300k = 0.80.


The Esmeraldas refinery (Ecuador's main refinery, 110k bpd capacity) has faced multiple shutdowns due to narco threats and equipment failures — another oil-narco feedback loop.

Fusarium TR4 — The Banana Existential Risk

Fusarium oxysporum Tropical Race 4 (TR4) is a soil-borne fungal pathogen that attacks Cavendish banana varieties (the world's dominant commercial banana). It destroyed the previous global variety (Gros Michel) in the 1950s and has been spreading globally since the 1990s from Southeast Asia. No cure exists once soil is infected — fields must be abandoned for decades.

Any confirmed TR4 outbreak in Ecuador's main banana production zones (Guayas, El Oro, Los Ríos) would be a KC_05 score of 0.75+ and trigger immediate DOLE bearish signal (0.85 magnitude). DOLE has direct procurement from Ecuador — the world's #1 banana exporter — making it the highest-conviction Ecuador impact stock.

Intelligence Narrative — Clerk Voice

The Canary No Other Model Has Built: Ecuador's Dollarized War State

The standard sovereign risk model does not have a bucket for Ecuador. Most frameworks treat dollarization as a stabilizing factor — and on inflation and exchange rate dimensions, it is. But the CivilisationOS ECRI framework is built around a different insight: dollarization eliminates the monetary escape valve, concentrating all risk in the fiscal and political channels. Ecuador cannot inflate away debt. It cannot devalue to restore competitiveness. When the fiscal position deteriorates — as it has in nine of the last eleven years — the only options are structural adjustment, external borrowing, or default. Ecuador has chosen default twice in five years.

The narco risk layer compounds this in ways unique to any canary in the 56-domain network. The Los Lobos and Choneros organizations do not merely threaten Ecuador's political stability — they threaten the physical infrastructure through which Ecuador's fiscal revenue flows. Guayaquil port handles approximately 70% of Ecuador's export volume. Esmeraldas refinery processes the crude that funds the government. When narco organizations establish effective control over these nodes — through extortion, infiltration, and armed presence — they become sovereign fiscal actors without legal standing. The Noboa government's "Internal Armed Conflict" declaration was the correct analytical recognition of this reality: this is not ordinary crime. It is a contested sovereignty over infrastructure.

The Yasuni ITT referendum adds a third destabilizing layer that is analytically pure and irreversible. Ecuador's voters democratically chose environmental protection over fiscal revenue — a rational preference aggregation that nonetheless accelerates the fiscal compression. The ~170,000 bpd of production potential locked beneath Yasuní is not coming back on a political timescale. Oil production is declining from 545k bpd (2019 peak) toward the 400k bpd KC_03 threshold, below which scoring escalates sharply. We are 30k bpd from that threshold at current trajectory.

The IMF programme (2023–2026, ~$4B) is the primary credibility anchor and the most important stabilizing variable in the current ECRI. If tranches begin to slip — triggered by fiscal slippage, political disruption from narco violence, or structural reform failures — KC_04 would escalate rapidly and push ECRI toward ELEVATED. Ecuador's creditors have seen this movie twice. Third-time default risk is priced into the 12.8% bond yield premium.

For US equity markets, the DOLE signal is unique in the network: no other canary domain has a US-listed company with such direct and concentrated supply chain exposure to a single WATCH-zone country. Dole Food's Ecuador banana procurement is not marginal — Ecuador is the world's largest banana exporter, and Dole is the world's largest banana company. A KC_02 port capture event simultaneously triggers a DOLE supply chain disruption signal. A KC_05 Fusarium TR4 outbreak triggers a DOLE existential-level supply shock. Both signals are operationally real, not theoretical.

ECRI 0.4350 WATCH — THE CANARY IS WATCHING. Ecuador is 11.5 points below the WATCH/ELEVATED boundary. KC_02 is 12 points below the single trigger threshold. The trajectory is gradual compression toward the boundary, not rapid escalation — unless a discrete event (port capture, assassination, IMF suspension) fires the single trigger mechanism. Monitor continuously.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1800  NORMAL

Banco Central del Ecuador operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Panama Canal (significant dependency, weight 0.40).

Critical import dependencies include industrial machinery, consumer electronics, refined fuels, industrial chemicals. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — crude oil, bananas, shrimp, cocoa — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1800 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Panama Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

Banco Central del Ecuador is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Ecuador real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Ecuador are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3900  WATCH

Banco Central del Ecuador faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Earthquake, Flood, Volcanic Eruption. High-severity events (Earthquake) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Ecuador is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.3900 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Earthquake event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
